summary: Recent battlefield reports highlight multiple drone engagements and tactical developments. On the Donetsk front, a Russian reconnaissance drone "Furiiya" was shot down before transmitting data, while on the Sumy sector, a strike by the "Lyutyy" drone on an important target was prevented. A Russian Ka-27 helicopter was destroyed by an FPV drone strike on the "Sivash" drilling platform in the Black Sea, with drones launched from a BUK system and controlled via Starlink. The "Rubikon" Center reported 128 enemy targets destroyed on March 5, 2026, including Ukrainian Armed Forces personnel, a 2S22 "Bogdan" self-propelled howitzer, an M109, and a T-64BV tank. Additionally, a FAB-3000 bomb strike with UMPK on Ukrainian positions in Krasny Liman was captured via an optical fiber FPV drone "KVN." Analysts note the rapid scaling of Ukrainian drone innovations by Russian forces and the growing importance of rotating antenna systems for counter-FPV drone electronic warfare.
